response.justification: The candidate is an experienced generalist software engineer with strong web, full-stack, and DLang/Node.js backgrounds, but the profile does not match this embedded networking security role. Native-level Japanese is a hard requirement and is entirely absent (only English, Romanian, and German listed). C proficiency for UNIX development is minimal—roughly six months at National Instruments and a thesis project—and C is not even in the skills list. There is no evidence of Linux kernel modification, network switch/L2-L3 technologies, or security domains like MACsec, 802.1X, or tamper detection. The embedded claims (robotic arms, device orchestration) are tangential rather than firmware/networking security work.
strengths: Long, varied software-development career well beyond the 3+ year minimum
Fluent English plus some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments
Strong testing/TDD discipline and high-throughput systems experience
concerns: No native-level (or any) Japanese proficiency—a hard requirement
No security or LAN-switch networking domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF/SNMP, L2/L3)
Minimal C and no Linux kernel source-modification experience

key_factors.factor: Weak C / no Linux kernel work
